sections.paragraph:
καταδιώκω fut. καταδιώξω LXX; 1 aor. κατεδίωξα; fut. mid. καταδιώξομαι LXX; aor. pass. 2 pl. κατεδιώχθητε Mi 2:11 (s. διώκω; Thu. et al.; pap, LXX; PsSol 15:8; TestSol 25:6; Test12Patr; JosAs; ApcEl [PSI 7 verso, 6] κατα[διώκουσιν], nearly always ‘pursue’ in a hostile sense; so also Theoph. Ant. 3, 21 [p. 246, 9] Φαραώ … κ. Ἑβραίους) search for eagerly, hunt for τινά someone Mk 1:36 (in a good sense in Polyb. 6, 42, 1; Ps 37:21. W. acc. of the pers. Ps 22:6. κ. μετά τινος=go w. someone 1 Km 30:22. ὀπίσω τινός Sir 27:17).—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

  1. pl. = plural

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ApcEl ApcEl = Apocalypse of Elijah—List 2

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 516
